title: redb use-case and authority contract bounded
objective: Inventory every current and proposed redb record class across CodeDB source blobs, envctl migration state, high-frequency buffers, caches, WAL/outbox, ingestion buffers, and shared read-views, and assign each a justified use case, owner, lifetime, durability requirement, identity, synchronization edge, crash-replay rule, recovery source, observability signal, and explicit relationship to PostgreSQL and CodeDB — under the settled STORE-001 tiered-authority model, so that no existing durable use is silently reclassified.

work_items.surface: PGREDB-001 — CodeDB source_blobs (codedb_store_redb, durable default today)
work_items.owner: CodeDB capture CLI (single writer per store file)
work_items.decision: Classify durable-TRANSITIONAL: target owner is PostgreSQL-canonical per STORE-001 'durable relational macro-state'; redb demotes to transient forward buffer only after a green live blobstore_parity.rs cutover under one preserved SHA-256 identity. Remains redb-durable until then. Reclassification is explicit and gated, not silent.

work_items.surface: PGREDB-002 — envctl migration_db (durable runtime store, ADOPT-002)
work_items.decision: Split classification: durable audit HISTORY targets PostgreSQL-canonical via forward promotion (STORE-001 'histories'); in-flight runtime orchestration state may remain redb-transient. Migration is explicit and identity-preserving via the replayable migration engine; kept distinct from CodeDB to avoid conflation.

work_items.owner: owning agent runtime (single writer per scratchpad)
work_items.decision: Classify transient-TERMINAL: redb is the STORE-001 canonical owner of transient high-frequency state (agent thoughts/traces/logs). Forward-only optional flush to PostgreSQL for significant states; loss-on-crash acceptable. No durable use to preserve; the microsecond-latency claim stays UNVERIFIED and is not asserted.

work_items.surface: PGREDB-004 — embedding/dedup cache + f32 geometry engine (proposed)
work_items.decision: Classify disposable CACHE: BLAKE3-keyed, miss-is-safe, never authority; the redb 'geometry engine' f32 distance-math role is the compute function over these cached vectors, not a distinct class. Canonical vectors are owned by PostgreSQL+RuVector (native extension, no sidecar); redb holds no vector/SQL authority.

Classify durable-until-ACKED buffer, strictly subordinate to PostgreSQL; distinct from PostgreSQL's own internal WAL. Forward-only replay-with-ack; PostgreSQL is destination authority (envctl is not the durable writer; AUTH-CLAIM-001 refuted). Ordering/idempotency/backpressure protocol is a required build item before adoption.

work_items.surface: PGREDB-006 — intermediate ingestion buffer (proposed target; contradicted current durable default)
work_items.decision: Resolve the direct contradiction via STORE-001: adopt the buffer-only framing (redb transient, parser->redb->envctl->PostgreSQL) as the TARGET, and route the current durable-on-redb default through PGREDB-001's verified cutover rather than silently flipping either class. Current reality (no plugin->redb buffering, NUPG-CLAIM-007) is the build gap, not asserted as done.

work_items.surface: PGREDB-007 — shared redb read-view for LifeOS UI over UDS (proposed)
work_items.owner: agents write (PGREDB-003 class); LifeOS UI reads
work_items.decision: Classify non-authoritative READ-VIEW: low-latency UI projection over transient agent/redb state; must never become a second writable owner. UDS-only vs shared-redb-file access is an owner decision deferred to PGAUTH-004. Holds no PostgreSQL or CodeDB authority.

Record every currently-durable redb use (PGREDB-001, PGREDB-002) in an explicit reclassification ledger with target owner, migration mechanism, verified cutover proof gate, and zero-loss guarantee (SHA-256 identity preserved). Hold all activation/migration for STORE-001's gated cutover proofs; this task performs planning classification only, no store mutation.
